name: My Project
shortDesc: One-paragraph pitch shown on the homepage card. Focus on the problem, not the solution. What's broken, what's painful, what makes someone go 'yeah I've felt that'?

hero.title: Headline that names the problem.
paragraphs: First paragraph: set the scene. What does the user feel, what's broken, what's the stakes?
Second paragraph: introduce the app. What does it do, in concrete terms?

points.title: Bold feature claim
points.desc: One or two sentences elaborating. Backticks turn into `code` styling.

points.title: Second feature claim
points.desc: Keep these to three or four items max. They're the highlight reel.

demo.label: Screenshots
demo.title: See it in action
demo.subtitle: One-line description of what the gallery shows.

screenshots.caption: Optional caption. Drop captions if the image speaks for itself.
Drop image files into public/screenshots/<slug>/ and reference them with an absolute path.

howItWorks.label: How it works
howItWorks.title: From start to finish
howItWorks.subtitle: Three steps from open to outcome.

steps.title: First step
steps.desc: What the user does and what the app does in response. Backticks render as `code`.

steps.title: Second step
steps.desc: Keep these focused on the user's experience, not internal architecture.

steps.title: Third step
steps.desc: End on the payoff. What the user walks away with.

tech.label: Under the hood
tech.title: How it was built
tech.subtitle: One line on the architecture choice.

items: Anything that would surprise a reader
